title: sapium lateriflorum
intro: Sapium lateriflorum, also known as the Indian cork tree or the Sapium tree, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in India and Southeast Asia. The bark, leaves, and roots of this tree have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Sapium lateriflorum include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating digestive issues, among others. The herbal preparations of Sapium lateriflorum include decoctions, infusions, and paste preparations, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
